meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Slow Dev
content.seo_description: Master rapid development as a student. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to accelerate your portfolio builds and ship production-ready SaaS.
content.answer_block: Slow development speed is the silent killer of student projects. The Antigravity template eliminates boilerplate, providing a production-grade foundation. By pairing this with Claude Code’s autonomous file manipulation, you slash setup time by 90%, allowing you to focus on high-value feature engineering rather than tedious manual scaffolding.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the weight of a complex feature set looming over your weekend. You have the vision, but the sheer volume of boilerplate code between you and your first commit feels like an insurmountable wall. Most students burn out here, trapped in the cycle of configuration errors and dependency hell.

But what if you could bypass the scaffolding phase entirely? The Antigravity project template acts as a high-velocity launchpad, abstracting away the mundane so you can operate at the level of a senior architect. Combined with Claude Code, you aren't just writing lines; you are orchestrating an entire system from the command line.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the foundational architecture. This step ensures your project has the necessary directory structures, pre-configured environment variables, and essential security middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project with authentication, PostgreSQL setup, and Stripe integration configured for a student-friendly local dev environment.

steps.title: Define Domain Models via Schema
steps.description: Use the template’s schema-first approach to define your database entities. This prevents drift between your database and your application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Prisma schema models for a User-Task-Project relationship with appropriate relations and indexing.

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe API Routes
steps.description: Leverage Antigravity’s built-in RPC capabilities to ensure your frontend and backend stay perfectly in sync without manual API documentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a type-safe RPC endpoint for fetching user data that handles pagination and error boundaries automatically.

steps.title: Automate UI Component Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't build buttons from scratch. Use the template's integrated component library to maintain visual consistency while accelerating development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard layout using the Antigravity design system components including a sidebar, header, and data grid.

steps.title: Execute End-to-End Testing
steps.description: Validate your entire stack. Automated testing is the difference between a prototype and a portfolio-ready SaaS product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test suite for the user authentication flow, verifying login, session persistence, and logout.

pro_tips: Always use the 'dry-run' flag in Claude Code to review proposed file changes before committing to complex architectural shifts.
Map your database models before writing UI code; Antigravity uses these models to auto-generate form validation schemas, saving hours of manual coding.
Leverage the template's built-in 'mock-mode' to develop your frontend UI even when the backend API is still under construction.
Use Claude Code to audit your dependency tree every week; keeping packages lean is vital for long-term project maintainability.
Configure your environment variables in a single .env.vault file to ensure your secrets are never committed to version control.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, push your repository to GitHub, document your architecture in a README.md, and start building your first core feature.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the tools to outpace the competition and build like a senior engineer. Stop wasting time on boilerplate and start focusing on the logic that makes your app unique. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
